Evaluation Theory, Models, and Applications

Book overview and chapter study-note index for Evaluation Theory, Models, and Applications.

Book notes

Evaluation Theory, Models, and Applications

Study notes on Evaluation Theory, Models, and Applications (Stufflebeam and Coryn 2014). These chapter notes are generated from source-linked MCP research-library records and are intended for study, comparison, and later refinement.

26 chapter notes 2014

Synthesis

Core Concepts

Recurring ideas and distinctions used across these chapter notes.

Map

Chapter Map

Full part-by-part reading route for all 26 chapters.

Practice

Practice Implications

Practical evaluation design habits drawn from the full chapter set.

Source

2014Book overviewChapter route

This index summarises generated study notes for Daniel L. Stufflebeam and Chris L. S. Coryn’s Evaluation Theory, Models, and Applications (Stufflebeam and Coryn 2014). The generated pages use selected MCP research-library retrieval records and compact evidence summaries.

Core Argument

Evaluation approaches should be chosen, adapted, and judged by their contribution to defensible value judgments, practical improvement, accountability, standards compliance, and use.

Concept Map

Chapter Notes

Part One: Fundamentals of Evaluation

  • Chapter 1: Overview of the Evaluation Field - source pages 39-79
  • Chapter 2: Evaluation Theory - source pages 81-102
  • Chapter 3: Standards for Program Evaluations - source pages 105-138

Part Two: An Evaluation of Evaluation Approaches and Models

  • Chapter 4: Background for Assessing Evaluation Approaches - source pages 143-150
  • Chapter 5: Pseudoevaluations - source pages 151-168
  • Chapter 6: Quasi-evaluation Studies - source pages 169-208
  • Chapter 7: Improvement- and Accountability-oriented Evaluation Approaches - source pages 209-226
  • Chapter 8: Social Agenda and Advocacy Evaluation Approaches - source pages 227-245
  • Chapter 9: Eclectic Evaluation Approaches - source pages 247-261
  • Chapter 10: Best Approaches for Twenty-first-century Evaluations - source pages 265-281

Part Three: Explication of Selected Evaluation Approaches

  • Chapter 11: Experimental and Quasi-experimental Design Evaluations - source pages 285-324
  • Chapter 12: Case Study Evaluations - source pages 327-342
  • Chapter 13: Daniel Stufflebeam’s CIPP Model for Evaluation - source pages 345-375
  • Chapter 14: Michael Scriven’s Consumer-oriented Approach to Evaluation - source pages 377-405
  • Chapter 15: Robert Stake’s Responsive or Stakeholder-centered Evaluation Approach - source pages 409-435
  • Chapter 16: Michael Patton’s Utilization-focused Evaluation - source pages 439-453

Part Four: Evaluation Tasks, Procedures, and Tools

  • Chapter 17: Identifying and Assessing Evaluation Opportunities - source pages 459-468
  • Chapter 18: First Steps in Addressing Evaluation Opportunities - source pages 471-479
  • Chapter 19: Designing Evaluations - source pages 481-510
  • Chapter 20: Budgeting Evaluations - source pages 515-539
  • Chapter 21: Contracting Evaluations - source pages 541-554
  • Chapter 22: Collecting Evaluative Information - source pages 555-579
  • Chapter 23: Analyzing and Synthesizing Information - source pages 593-624
  • Chapter 24: Communicating Evaluation Findings - source pages 625-662

Part Five: Metaevaluation and Institutionalizing and Mainstreaming Evaluation

  • Chapter 25: Metaevaluation: Evaluating Evaluations - source pages 667-705
  • Chapter 26: Institutionalizing and Mainstreaming Evaluation - source pages 707-725
